entry.title: FAGLGVTR run to old year by mistake 0205
entry.content:
Hi folks,

FAGLGVTR was run to transfer balance to year 0205 instead of 2025.

Now the transaction does not allow direct transfer to 2025, but wants to go through all the years 205-2025 🙂

How can we fix that, despite of LMSW?

Thanks

ILIAN

entry.title: Re: Exam Attempt

Tried but could not find option for india.

raised ticket with SAP team and they replied only NEFT payment accepted as some technical glitch there but still account details are in complete hence raised ticket again

entry.title: I am doing SAP SCM processes like MM SD WM PP and Sales force admin. Would I get some insights
entry.content:
I'd need some support and knowledge regarding the future and scope of what I am upto so far, as a beginner in the industry. 

I am being trained for SAP SCM with different processes like MM Wm pp and sd , in addition to sales force admin. 

May I have some insights regarding what would be the scope ahead and will that be a Good avenue to enter and grow ?

entry.title: Re: Custom Email Template for Email Integration for Purchase Requisition Approval in SAP S/4HANA

@dlb79 You must assign the mail template with app "Register Extensions" to a TR.

3069838 - How to transport Email Templates in SAP S/4HANA On-Premise? - SAP for Me

Try with start routine, where you can use If Condition to filter data on multiple objects.

Thanks.

Regards,

Anand

entry.title: Re: TM in SAP S/4HANA 2023: Maintain Carbon Emission on Vehicle Type
entry.content:
@JakubC 

Once the FO is eventually set to 'completed' the FU Items get their fair share of the GHG emission.

entry.title: Re: Querry Convert KPI to Characteristics
entry.content: Aru you using BW4HANA?

entry.title: Re: Parallel operations in IBP S&OP TS Based Heuristics
entry.content:
Hi,

there a no parallel operations in time-series based planning. But for your use-case you can assign the 4 resources to one production resource.

See also Production Source Resource and the first note there:
"If you assign more than one resource to a production source of supply, the consumption of these resources isn’t split. All of them are consumed as if there was only one resource. Assigning multiple resources to one source means that all the resources are consumed according to their respective consumption rates, that is, if one of the resources doesn’t have enough capacity, the source can’t be used."

Cheers, Carsten

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
hi @shraddha_karne it is great to meet you in the community! I live in the US outside of Philadelphia.

Be sure to check out the many Location Groups | SAP Community and the many interest groups, for example Enterprise Architecture - SAP Community.

May I ask your area of expertise and interest? Best of luck!

they replied saying buying via cart has been stopped and need to make only NEFT payment.

they provided with bank details but there is no account number in it. can someone reply if purchased attempt how you did so useful for many people here strugggling for solution.

I have Chatbot appliation. I have created the Docker compose file because it has two containers running on sideto make the application working. I have no idea how to deploy the application to SAP AI core and use an application via its endpoint.

Could anyone explain me this ?

Should I create an OData action or is there another way to send the data captured with the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-mobile-dev-kit-nativescript-geolocation.html to the database? Thank you very much for your help.

entry.title: Lumira Designer - Chart - static color to character
entry.content:
Hello,

we are using SAP Lumira Designer 22.0.3 on this mode:

And I use the normal chart type like my example:

If I filter the to "Mouse" for example, the color of this character and also

the Bar in the Chart changes to Blue.

My wish is, that the color for "Mouse" is everytime orange, no matter which filter is on the chart.

And for Bird the color is everytime Red and ...

Who has an idea what I have to do here?

Regards Marcus

entry.title: How to Change the Bank Statement Format
entry.content:
Hi!

We have a requirement to change the bank statement format configuration.

We will need to have the link between the external transaction - posting rule - acct symbol and G/L account to know how it will be the new postings with the new bank statement format.

Please, could you provide it to us?

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Change the Bank Statement Format
entry.content:
You need to make these changes to the Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statements (SSCUI 101024) setting.

Also in the bank account master data configuration, in the Manage Bank Accounts app, you have to change the field Payment Transaction Type Group. In case you don't put anything, it means you would be accepting any format.

And finally, in the same SSCUI 101024 (Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statements), you can see the link between bank account and transaction type.

This could be useful: 2854415 - Information on Bank Statement Posting Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Moving from Version 1 to Version 2 of the Enterprise Project OData API
entry.content:
As a complement, the deletion of Version 1 of Enterprise Project OData API is planned for the 2508 release.

Additional information regarding the Release Schedule and dates for 2025 can be found in the following link:

  • SAP Help Portal - Release Schedule and Dates for 2025.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:

3557622 - GARN: Adjustments to Figuring Amount Exempt from Levy (V_T5UGE) view

entry.title: Re: Smart and flexible: sap.ui.mdc released for productive use
entry.content:
Hi @lumencloud,

Are you looking for tutorials beyond the SAP-samples/ui5-mdc-json-tutorial? We're currently developing additional steps that involve advanced UI5 features, which integrate well with sap.ui.mdc.

At the moment, we don't have plans for more tutorials. However, if you could specify any open questions you have after following the mentioned tutorial, we can consider creating additional content to address those.

In any case, I recommend checking out the Flexible Programming Model Explorer for OData V4 apps. This can help you avoid reimplementing features already covered by the building blocks.

NB: if you copy images or texts from others, you should give credit (that doesn't prove they are the authors though), make sure to obtain the authorization/copyright. For SAP, I guess there is no issue in posting public images but add the link. Images concerned:

entry.title: In CC01, I can not add object types
entry.content:
Hi,

When I record my changes without the approval key, the object types that are open are closed when I do it with the approval key.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Using Customer-Defined Events in Business Event Logging
entry.content:
You can use Business Event Logging (BEL) to log SAP-delivered events, SAP-extended events, or your events (custom events). 

You can find more information about Business Event Logging here:             

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment  
  • Business Event Logging SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud (Latest) 
  • Business Event Logging SAP S/4 Hana Private Cloud (Latest)                                                        

 There are two ways to define and log custom events in Business Event Logging: 

  • Extend SAP Event 
  • Add Customer Event to SAP Object or Define Customer Event for Customer Object 

Extend SAP Event 

In some scenarios, the information provided by an SAP-delivered event may not suffice to meet business requirements. In such cases, there is a need to augment the event payload with more information. You can use the concept of derived events in the RAP framework to provide more information in the SAP-delivered event. The Business Event Logging framework supports the logging of such extended events. You can refer to the links below to understand how to implement extension via derived events: 

  •   Derived Events 
  •   Prerequisite for Derived Events 

The BEL framework provides an out-of-the-box feature to log all extended events, thereby cutting the need for any manual intervention. You must ensure the framework is active for the given SAP Object Type. 

This functionality is available on: 

         ABAP on BTP (Steampunk) (Latest) 

        SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition (Latest) 

        SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2023 Onwards) 

        SAP S/4 HANA OP 2023 onwards  

Add Customer Event to SAP Object or Define Customer Event for Customer Object 

Business Event Logging has out-of-the-box support for the scenario wherein a customer extends an SAP-delivered Business Object with a Custom event or in pure custom development. RAP has information about how an event can be modeled and raised. You can find the implementation details here. 

Once you have defined an event in RAP Behavior implementation class, you must follow certain prerequisite tasks to ensure that the events are logged in the Business Event Logging framework. 

  1. It is mandatory to add the annotation ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name in the abstract entity with the correct SAP Object Node type. The annotation must be in camel case.               

 

     2   In case the event is defined at the root node in BDEF (and not at the correct sub-node), we must add the SAP  Object Node Type Keys (Event.sapObjectNodeTypeKey) via annotations in the Abstract Entity. The order of the keys must be preserved here.     

               

 

   This functionality is available on: 

         ABAP on BTP (Steampunk) (Latest) 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition (Latest) 

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2022 FPS2 onwards for Pure Custom Scenario)   

         SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition (Release 2023 onwards for SAP Delivered Event Extension)  

         SAP S/4 HANA OP 2022 FPS2 onwards (Pure Custom Scenario)  

          SAP S/4 HANA OP 2023 onwards (For SAP Delivered Event Extension)  

 

Customer Events in the Event Log 

 

 

All SAP-delivered business events use the producer event namespace “sap.s4.beh”.  The Customer events have a different customer provider namespace, according to their definition in the RAP event binding. You can also filter the events by producer event namespace. 

 

Refer to the below mentioned blog to get an overview of Business Event Log (BEL) framework. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/one-tool-to-track-them-all/ba-p/13957191

entry.title: Re: Existing RFC for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 t-c
entry.content:
A standard SAP system has millions of RFC-enabled function modules... I think without the knowhow of an expert from the relevant application area, there is no chance to find the requested FMs or OData interfaces (if they exist...)

I have searched the SAP tags for "Process Director", but didn't find anything. So to which SAP application area does the transaction "/cockpit/1" belong?

The SAP tags you added so far, are all "basis technology" areas. They won't be able to help with that question. (It's like asking someone who works in a factory that produces mobile phones, whether he can tell you the phone number of the person you want to call... -- Just because someone knows how to manufacture a phone, doesn't mean that he knows the phone numbers of millions & millions of people... 🙂 )

entry.title: How to set up Filters in AIFAEM
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Advanced Event Mesh, there is the possibility to apply filters from consumer side based on the Topic. For example:

Topic: sap/aif/businesspartner/change/<BusinessPartnerGroup>

One Consumer could be only interested in one Group and would subscribe to

sap/aif/businesspartner/change/0001

Other consumers might want to consume everything and subscribe to 

sap/aif/businesspartner/change/>

In some cases, the SAP Event Source might want to filter on Sender Side in order to only send a Subset of the Data in the System. This is possible in AIFAEM and I will show you one way to do it.

Target Message Structure

In my first blog on this topic I have created a simple Business Partner Data Event. Now let´s assume we want to filter based on the Business Partner Group. Since I didn´t have this field in my structure, I simply added it into my Structure:

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'ABUSINESSPARTNER but with less fields'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
define structure zaifaem_abusinesspartner {

  businesspartner         : abap.string(0);
  businesspartnergrouping : abap.string(0);
  firstname               : abap.string(0);
  lastname                : abap.string(0);

}

 

 

I also added it into my Structure Mapping Function Module

Also, I want to add another Field to the Target Structure. This filed will control, if an event is sent out or not.

if IGNORE = "X", we want to ignore this Event and not send it to AEM.

 

Value Mapping Definition

This Value Mapping is intended with a Whitelisting Approach. Unless I maintain something else in the Value Mapping Table, It will map the Default Value "X". For Group 0001 I have maintained an entry with "" (empty), so IGNORE will be empty.

TCode: /N/AIF/VMAP

 

If you want to do a Blacklisting Appraoch, you just need to remove the Default Value. Then the Value Mapping will map "" as default. Then you can Add Entries with "X" in the right Column and will set IGNORE = "X" for those.

The Value Mapping of Course has to be implemented in the Structure Mapping. Since I want to use a Field from the Target Structure, which will be available after the Structure Mapping 20 (DATA), I added a Structure Mapping 30 to ZAIF_AEM_BUPA_EVENT_MESSAGE

Then I add the Field Mapping for "IGNORE"

 

Check Definition

In the Action I want the AIF to check if the Field "IGNORE" is empty. If yes, it should send the Event to AEM. If not, it should not send it. Therefore I defined a Check in /N/AIF/CUST -> Define Check

For the Warning message just define a message in any message class in SE91

 

Action 

In /N/AIF/CUST -> Define Actions you will need to add the Target Message Type as Main Component Type

For the Standard Action /AIF/AIF_ACTION_CALL_AEM we add this Check

 

Test

Now let´s Test. 

 

1) Business Partner which should be sent --> 104

2) Business Partner which shouldn´t be sent --> 103

The Info Message we only see with Trace Level 2. You can enable it here:

--> Mass Maintain

3) Other Interfaces

Since we are using the Action for all Interfaces, we have to make sure that other Interfaces without Filter still work. For instance I have another Event for SalesOrders without the "IGNORE" Field:

Expecation is, that AIF will not find the Field and therefore the Check is skipped and the Event is sent out. Let´s try: Changing Sales Order in VA02

AIF:

entry.title: Re: How to work with test cases and test plans in several...
entry.content:
Hi Aj - Can you please let us know when the test plan copy feature is planned to release? 

Use Case Scenario is,

- We have defined our test plan per domain for the release 2402 (scope)

- We would like to copy the same test plan for the release 2502 (scope). So that we don't want to build everything from scratch again.

Also I dont see an option for bulk export or bulk import as alternative option. Appreciate your help on this. Thanks!

entry.title: Apache Kafka Connection from BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have requirement to connect to Apache Kafka from BTP Integration Suite and then transform those XML messages and push them to SAP Backend systems. Could you please let me know if there are any pre-requisites needed to connect to Apache Kafka using Integration Suite. I knew that there is a standard Kafka adapter to connect to Apache Kafka but still any important points to consider before building interface on SAP Integration Suite? We mainly need support to connect to Apache Kafka .. What details should we ask Apache Kafka to share? Should we use connect to Apache Kafka using cloud Connector? Any settings needed to done on Apache Kafka side also? Also should we raise Firewall clearance?

Thanks

Ramu.

entry.title: Re: Everything About SAP Idocs in S/4 HANA

entry.title: Re: Learning administrator privileges - Auto Generate [It...
entry.content: @DavidRaykovitz, as I understand it, this function isn't currently available. Enhancement requests (link1, link2) were closed due to lack of votes. A new one could be created and voted (KBA 2090228), where the Learning Product Management team will further review and assess the requested functionality.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - RBSC portal "500 Internal Server Error"
entry.content:
Please check the following SAP Note:

3326425 - "Go to RBSC portal" of SAP Build Process Automation end with error "500 Internal Server Error"

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003326425

entry.title: Determine which is the correct component for certain report executed by a Job
entry.content:
When working with a SAP System and finding errors for certain job under job log section, if you are not able to run troubleshooting on corresponding issue and want to contact SAP to check possible solutions, we recommend you to review which is the best SAP Component to be selected.

As an example, we make usage of one SAP Solution Manager job named SM:LMDB GENERIC UPLOAD.

The job for some reason is presenting cancelled status, then you check job logs and you are not able to identify which is the exact issue, and want to contact SAP to assist:

Through job logs you can check which is the report failing in our example:

Step 001 started (program RLMDB_UPLOAD_BACKGROUND, variant , user ID XX_XXXX)

With this information, you can access transaction SE38 to review which is the associated package which will provide the Application Component information:

Once you know the exact Software Component, when creating an incident in SAP For Me, you can edit the corresponding Software Component to the correct one in Submit Section:


In component are click edit button and add the correct Software Component, so it reaches the correct SAP Technical Support team for further analysis:

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - RBSC portal "500 Internal Server Error"
entry.content:
Also check the following:

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-teched-discussions/sap-teched-2022-ai180-ad182-prerequisites-setup-desktop-agent-3-rbsc-portal/m-p/12624545#M2361

entry.title: Re: sap.m.radiobutton circle not visible
entry.content: Do you have data binding for the not working one?

entry.title: Can SAP provide a pop up message to use a new materialcode as the one used is marked for deletion?
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have a material marked for deletion, the new material is ready for use. Upon entering the mfd code in the sales order, is SAP able to provide a pop up message, saying to use the new code instead?   

Thank you and wish you all still the best for 2025.

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content:
I did that several times and no matter what I tried if the new dimension I added had 'D' in the @prompt function if I added it to a query and tried to run it I would an error. Explained and shared several times in this thread but once more now;
The following database error occurred: [Cache ODBC][State : 22007][Native Code 22007] [E:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\WIReportServer.exe] ERROR #388: Unknown error, code 22007. For information about this error, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2054721 on the SAP Support Portal. (IES 10901)
I finally got it to work by tying it as CAST(@Prompt('DatePrompt','A',,Mono,Free,Not_Persistent,{'1900-01-01'}) AS Date)

entry.title: Re: Exciting news for SAP SuccessFactors Learning in 2H2023: the new integrated learning experience
entry.content: Does anyone know why the manager experience wouldn't load? We've turned on the integrated settings for the new learner experience, but the manager experience no longer loads the data properly to track their team's progress on required assignments.

entry.title: Re: FAGLGVTR run to old year by mistake 0205
entry.content:
You should first read KBA 2527328 - Year-End-Closing in Asset Accounting (NEW): Best Practice - Year-End Closing in Asset Accounting (NEW)

If you haven't found a solution, or if you haven't received a response from someone who has made the same mistake, consider create an issue. (personally, I wouldn't take a chance with T093C-LGJHAR without support)

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: Hi, have you found a solution for this? I recently found about this issue (today). I was calling the MODIFY ENTITIES of a released RAP BO (material document and purchase order) in the save_modified method and it suddenly crash. Mine is a managed RAP BO with unmanaged save, and as per documentation, we cannot call the MODIFY ENTITIES in the SAVE_MODIFIED method, and we can only call them during the interaction phase or during the finalize method, which I cannot redefine since mine is a managed RAP BO. You mentioned you we able to call MODIFY ENTITY but not the COMMIT ENTITY?

entry.title: Data truncation with export to pdf
entry.content:
Hi There,

With our previous request on this PDF formatting issues and the resolution given by SAP team like service pack upgrade, we still could see formatting issues even with this upgrade. And as we mentioned earlier it's working in developer machine and failing (data truncation issues) in server machine, and now we figured out this problem is server machine is due to a font size increase in the generated PDF.

And also found a similar ticket with data truncation issues but we are unclear to make the suggested changes at our side since there some differences in Windows System Registry.

Can someone assist us to achieve a solution to our current problem (data truncation) in PDF.

Thanks and best regards.

entry.title: Asset Under Construction
entry.content:
Hello, I have the below instructions regard fixed assts and Assets under construction : 

  1. AS01: Creating an asset number(gives you an asset number)
  2. KO01 and KO02: Creating an investment internal order(gives you an order number to give to purchasing)
  3. MIRO: Post the invoice
  4. KO88: Settlement of internal order to asset (moving the posting to the asset)
  5. AIAB and AIBU (only for assets under construction): Changing an asset under construction to a normal asset (capitalising) 

There appears to be a step being missed somewhere as i have completed the first 3 actions and when i go to KO88, i have the below error : "Either the sender is already settled, or there is nothing to settle" 

When I enter transaction AIAB, i have the following error: " Asset not yet capitalised" and AIBU error is " Line item settlement is not possible for asset: VSS 100996 " 

 

Could anyone offer any advise or support with this please #S4Hana

entry.title: Re: Background job created date
entry.content: yes but I need the exact date when that was created not the last modified

entry.title: Re: New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know
entry.content:
Hi Sanjay, yes, the answer refers to the email solution for Germany.

Best regards, Monika

entry.title: Re: Installing lean Eclipse with ADT
entry.content:
Groundhog day again, is it?

When I did a new installation using Platform Runtime Binary 4.34 and then try to install matching ADT 2024-12 (3.46) I currently get the following error message

Cannot complete the install because one or more required items could not be found.
Software being installed: ABAP Development Tools for Web Dynpro (Developer Edition) 1.196.0 (com.sap.adt.wda.core.devedition.feature.group 1.196.0)
Missing requirement: ABAP Communication Framework 3.46.0 (com.sap.adt.communication 3.46.0) requires 'osgi.bundle; com.google.gson [2.11.0,3.0.0)' but it could not be found
Cannot satisfy dependency:
From: Controller 1.196.0 (com.sap.adt.wda.controller 1.196.0)
To: osgi.bundle; com.sap.adt.communication [3.46.0,4.0.0)

Cannot satisfy dependency:
From: ABAP Development Tools for Web Dynpro (Developer Edition) 1.196.0 (com.sap.adt.wda.core.devedition.feature.group 1.196.0)
To: org.eclipse.equinox.p2.iu; com.sap.adt.wda.core.feature.group [1.196.0,1.196.0]

Cannot satisfy dependency:
From: ABAP Development Tools for Web Dynpro 1.196.0 (com.sap.adt.wda.core.feature.group 1.196.0)
To: org.eclipse.equinox.p2.iu; com.sap.adt.wda.controller [1.196.0,1.196.0]

So basically com.google.gson is missing. I found, that I had this on my old machine under eclipse\plugins and I'm positive that I used Platform Runtime Binary there.

On a new machine / installation it does not seem to be delivered with the Platform Runtime Binary anymore. I is still delivered by the sdk, e.g. "eclipse-SDK-4.34-win32-x86_64.zip" though.

So using a Platform Runtime Binary may (currently) not be the best option to chose.

This may of course also be a bug at SAP's ADT or an artefact at my side but I figured It is worth sharing.

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content: There isn't a prompt in the dimension. When you use SQL Assistant and select your DatePrompt parameter, it will just add Prompt(DatePrompt). You do not need to code the prompt syntax.

Note that I'm customer (not partner)

entry.title: Re: Securing external RFC connections, including certificate-based authentication
entry.content:
Hi,

Sure, you can safely de-activate the user's password. Moreover, one of the screenshots even shows that logging in with a password is not allowed. 

Lukasz

entry.title: Re: Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM: how to configure an on-premise Selenium Runner

I am trying to config runner file in SUM, where I am getting "Webdriver Check: Runner address is not reachable" error. 

pls reply to the following queries.

1. do we need to use the same host as virtual host where we are configuring selenium webdriver infrastructure ?

2. what type of connection does the server and cloud connector should have.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Background job created date
entry.content: Check table TBTCO (AFAIK nothing available in some change documents or data changes log)

entry.title: Re: New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know
entry.content: Interesting - Thanks for your reply. Does email solution also requires registration of Peppol network or it can be done without that.

entry.title: Re: Orchestrate your Generative AI workflows through the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Is it in the roadmap that we can use grounding orchestration step with an existing HANA Could vector source ?
I mean, in my use case, I have already developed a pipeline that ingest documents from an on-premise Confluence source and store embedding into an HANA Cloud instance. It is a pity that I can not use grounding orchestration step with this existing document embedding.

entry.title: Re: How to add Snowflake as a source for SAP Datasphere or SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@Sefan_Linders  and @javierc 

more details from jdbc trace:

2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.core.HttpUtil FINE executeRequest:746 - Executing request with OCSP enabled: {}
2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.jdbc.SnowflakeDatabaseMetaData FINEST getPrimaryKeys:2079 - public ResultSet getPrimaryKeys(String catalog=null, String schema=L_CSSFC
001_E, String table=KNOWLEDGE__KAV)
2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.jdbc.SnowflakeConnectionV1 FINEST isClosed:298 - boolean isClosed()
2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.jdbc.SnowflakeConnectionV1 FINEST createStatement:484 - Statement createStatement(int resultSetType, int resultSetConcurrency)
2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.core.HttpUtil FINE executeRequestInternal:806 - Pool: [leased: 0; pending: 0; available: 3; max: 300] Executing: POST https://<hostname>.snowflakecomputing.com:443/telemetry/send HTTP/1.1

ultSetHoldability

2025-01-10 15:22:28.160 n.s.c.core.SFStatement FINEST <init>:83 - SFStatement(SFSession session)

TTP/1.1, retry timeout: 1,000, socket timeout: 300,000, max retries: 0, inject socket timeout: 0, canceling: null, without cookies: false, include retry parameters:
false, include request guid: true, retry http 403: false, no retry: false

T https://<hostname>.snowflakecomputing.com:443/telemetry/send HTTP/1.1

imaryKeys() */ primary keys in account

e32acf-732b-4344-862a-aa4a623f6c8d

nt

entry.title: Re: Asset Under Construction
entry.content: You could look at guides such as Performing Asset Accounting Processes in SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08
entry.content:
@simpataraDuring the presentations, it was promised that the slides would be made available. Can you please do that?
Thanks, Stephan

entry.title: Re: Modification of External user home page Workzone advance
entry.content: any suggestions or inputs ?

entry.title: Cannot Install Crystal reports 2016 on Windows 11
entry.content:
Sorry if this is not the right place but would appreciate any advice on this, thanks.

I can`t install Crystal Reports 2016 on my Windows 11 laptop. I have tried it on a Windows 8.1 desktop it is fine.
This is what I`ve done to try and get it working:
1. Set to full access the folder C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects and install folder c:\BOE
2. Renamed C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine_temp to setup.engine (this ensures that the install fails later than before with a Failed to update cache for execution error. If I don`t do this I get a Failed to update setup engine executables error).
3. Run as Admin
4. Rename the "C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\setup.exe" to "setup.exe.old"
5. Emptied User Temp folder

Log files:
ErrosandWarnings.log: Error: Could not move temp dest folder to dest folder.
setupengine.log - same error

If I don`t carry out step 2. above then
ErrosandWarnings.log: Can`t rename: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine_temp to: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\InstallData\setup.engine
setupengine.log - same error

There is a webpage explaining the Failed to update cache for execution error:
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1781641
but I get 401 Not Authorized (when logged in) if I click on Read more...

entry.title: Confirm produced goods via SOAP API
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have created a Python application with which production employees at the packing station can “count” the packaged, finished goods and save the values. Now I would like to report the finished quantities directly from the application to SAP.
I have already implemented the search for production lots and the production tasks associated with the production lot are retrieved and displayed correctly. Now I have tried various update requests (as described in the documentation), but I am not able to confirm the confirmed quantities.

Can someone please tell me the exact steps I need to take to implement this via SOAP API? Presumably there is a “logic pattern” that needs to be followed. At best, someone can list the necessary steps and give me an example request.

I am new here and hope you understand.

Best regards
Simon

entry.title: Re: Joule Hana modeling assistant not working in SAP Buil...
entry.content:
Hi,

a new version of the modeling extension was deployed recently. Please check if it works now.

Best regards,

Jonathan

entry.title: Maintenance Plan vs Item
entry.content: I have 20 pieces of identical equipment that share a task list with time based strategy. I made a maintenance plan that lists all 20 equipment in it's object list.  I need each object to have it's own monthlies staggered from each other, can I do that with ONE Maintenance Item, or do I need to make a maintenance item for EACH object/equipment? Please advise

entry.title: SAP Codejam 2025 in Herne
entry.content:
hello, we would like to organize a SAP Codejam.

  • location: Herne
  • topic: Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub
  • maximum number of people: 25 people
  • when: from April 2025

we would be happy to host another sap codejam

entry.title: Re: SAP Forms service by Adobe in SAP BTP, ABAP environment
entry.content:
Hi Pascal, 

     Thanks for the great Blog! I was able to execute the entire process with plain and simple CDS views. However, when I am using CDS views with Parameters in the association, getting the below error when executing the get template method.

"Client Error: Navigation from parameter entity 'ZI_PROJECT_DATES' via 'Set' or 'Result' is not used" 

here ZI_Project is the main entity and association to ZI_PROJECT_DATES (this is a parameterized view) is defined. However am getting this error. Any inputs?

Thanks & Regards,

Raja Sekhar

entry.title: Re: Existing RFC for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 t-c
entry.content: Process Director is software by Tungsten Automation (previously known as Kofax or ReadSoft) based on MM and FI modules mainly (as far as I know).

entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content:
Good morning/afternoon,

Can you please clarify what you call "values"? Is that the previous or default answers or the values associated to a List Of Values (LOV)?

Regards

entry.title: Re: Existing RFC for Process Director, particularly for accessing data related to the /cockpit/1 t-c
entry.content: Ok, I have added MM and FIN, perhaps it helps...

entry.title: Re: Background job created date
entry.content:
Hi user,

You can check by 

1.Check Job Log
Go to Transaction SM37.


Enter your selection criteria (Job Name, User, etc.) and execute.
Select the desired job from the list.
Click on Job Log.
Check the first entry in the log. It typically shows when the job was created or started.


2. Check Job Details in SM37
In SM37, select your job and click on Job > Display Definition from the menu.
Check the Created by and Created on details at the top of the screen.

3. In SE16N also you check 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful!

Thanks.

entry.title: Implementing Credit Check in Service Orders for Enhanced Financial Control
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's competitive business environment, maintaining a healthy financial standing is critical for sustained growth and success. One crucial aspect of financial stability is effective credit management. Companies often rely on credit sales to stimulate business, but extending too much credit increases the risk of bad debt and financial loss. To mitigate these risks, implementing a robust credit check system in service orders becomes imperative.

This blog will explore how to enable credit checks within service orders, utilizing SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR). By leveraging these functionalities, businesses can gain greater control over their receivables, mitigate risks, and optimize their relationships with business partners. We will delve into the significance of credit management, the benefits of integrating credit checks in service orders, and provide a step-by-step guide to implementation.

Importance of Credit Management:

Effective credit management is pivotal for several reasons:

  1. Risk Mitigation: Proper credit checks help identify and manage potential risks associated with extending credit to customers who might default on payments.
  2. Cash Flow Management: Ensuring timely payments from customers enhances cash flow, enabling businesses to meet their financial obligations and invest in growth opportunities.
  3. Customer Relationship Management: Balancing credit availability while ensuring prompt payments helps build trust and foster long-term business relationships.

Before we dive into the specifics of enabling credit checks in service orders, let's understand the overarching functionality of SAP Credit Management and its role in safeguarding your company's financial health.

By setting the stage with the importance of credit management and its impact on business operations, readers will gain a clear understanding of why enabling credit checks in service orders is beneficial. With this context, we can now move on to the detailed functionality of SAP Credit Management.

Functionality:

Credit management is used to minimize financial risk through controlling and monitoring the credit allocations of your customers. The credit manager defines a credit amount for each customer, which can directly impact any transaction document. The creditworthiness and payment behavior of your business partners have an immediate effect on the business results of your company. Efficient receivables and credit management not only reduces the risk of financial losses but also helps optimize business relationships with your partners.

SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR) supports your company in determining the risk of losses on receivables from your business partners early on and making credit decisions efficiently and sometimes automatically. It also supports a heterogeneous and distributed system landscape; through system-independent XML interfaces, you can connect external non-SAP systems.

With SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR), you can operate centralized credit management. Applications such as Sales and Distribution, Logistics Execution, and Financial Accounting report the commitments of a business partner to SAP Credit Management. These reports are then consolidated into the credit exposure profile in SAP Credit Management and checked against the business partner's current credit limit. In addition to the credit limit check, you can perform other assessments, such as checking for the oldest open item, maximum dunning level, or the last payment.

By setting the stage with the importance of credit management and its impact on business operations, readers will gain a clear understanding of why enabling credit checks in service orders is beneficial. With this context, we can now move on to the detailed functionality of SAP Credit Management. SAP Credit Management supersedes FI-AR and this blog outlines setup and usage of SAP Credit Management in S/4HANA Service Applications.

This step-by-step guide is to show you how to enable FSCM Credit Management (SAP Credit Management) in S/4HANA Service: 

Customizing Credit Check

The following customizing will enable the credit check in Service Order. 

1. Define Credit Control Area:  From a credit management perspective, the credit control area is the highest organizational unit to which one or more company codes are assigned. The currency for all customers should be the same as the currency for the credit control area. An organizational unit that represents the area where the customer credit limit is awarded and monitored. A single Credit Control Area can span multiple company codes. Transaction ( OB45 ) maps Credit Control Area to Risk Category. 

 IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service->Define Credit Control Area.

2. Assign Sales Area to Credit Control Area: You use this activity to assign a sales area to a credit control area. A sales area- that is, a combination of the sales organization, distribution channel, and divisions- is assigned to a credit control area. Some sales areas not relevant for credit management may remain unassigned- for example, a sales area used for stock transfer. 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Assign Sales Area to Credit Control Area.

3. Define Credit Segment: In FSCM, the credit segment is the organizational unit for monitoring a customer's credit.  Credit Segment are required for the determination of the credit limit. 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service->Define Credit Segment 

4. Assign Credit Control Area and Credit Segment: All company codes for which credit management is required are assigned to one or another credit control area. Note that one company can be assigned only to one credit control area. 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Assign Credit Control Area and Credit Segment 

5. Define Risk Class. Based on the credit score, you can determine a risk class. The Risk Class should be the same as the Risk Categories that you define in the sales and distribution functionality. You use Transaction UKM_RISK_CLASS to define the range of scores that will automatically determine the Risk Class of the customer.               

6. Combine credit control area with risk category.  Customer from the same credit control area may have different reputation, some is good, but some is bad. To have different policies for them, we assign different risk class to different partner. Then we have the combination of credit control area and risk category.  

Risk Category: In FSCM, credit categories are used to identify the origin of a particular credit exposure amount.  
IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management-> Credit Management-> Integration with Sales and Distribution-> Define Risk Categories 

7. Define and Assign credit control areas to credit segments: This task is done using Transaction UKM_SEGMENT. You can assign one or more credit control areas to a credit segment

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Define Credit Segment 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Assign Credit Control Area and Credit Segment 

8. Define Credit Exposure Categories: 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Define Credit Exposure Categories.

9.Deactivate SAP Credit Management: 

IMG->Finance Supply Chain Management->Credit Management->Integration with Service-> Deactivate SAP Credit Management .

10. Enable Credit Check button in Service Order Transaction Type. 

11. Enable Credit Check Button in Item Category: Some materials (or services) may not be relevant for a credit check and thus do not update credit exposure. Such materials, such as promotional items or returnable packaging, due to tax law or company policy may not be billed free of cost. The item categories of such materials are not activated for a credit limit. 

Alternatively, the fastest way to do this is from IMG->Service->Transaction->Basic Settings->Credit Management 

Business Partner Credit Master Data 

You manage all credit-relevant master data of a business partner in SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR). This data includes the current credit limit, one or more externally determined rating values, the risk class, and an order limit. Each of the master data fields has a change history that gives information about the time of the change, the user, and the old and new value. There is also a note function that the credit analyst can use to enter additional information about the business partner. Alternatively, he or she can use a document storage function to store information such as press articles, correspondence, bank information, and rating information.  

 

  1. Configure business partner additional information: Additional information on collateral, negative credit events for a specific business partner, credit exception, and credit insurance are maintained using Transaction UKM_INFOCAT.  
  2. Create rules for credit score and credit limit: The rule that determines a score is maintained in the Rules field. Using Transaction UKM_LIMIT_RULE .
  3. Create formulas for credit score and credit limit: you can customize rules. You use Transaction UKM_FORMULAS to create customized formulas for rules. Using Formula Editor simulation, highlighted in Figure , you can edit standard formulas.  
  4. Risk Class: Based on the credit score, you can determine a risk class. The Risk Class should be the same as the Risk Categories that you define in the sales and distribution functionality. You use Transaction UMK_RISK_ CLASS  to define the range of scores that will automatically determine the Risk Class of the customer. 
  5. Activate a business partner role for credit management in SAPS/4HANA: In Transaction BUSI, the application UKM is activated, as shown in Figure:

     

  6. Once this application is activated, you can create the business partner role UKM000 (SAP Credit Management) using Transaction BP or UKM BP, as shown previously in Figure,UKM_BP is equivalent to FD32 
    T-code: BP or UKM_BP_SEGMENT 

  7. Check Rule: The Check Rule maintained in this field determines which check steps are valid for the customer and what the threshold limit is for each individual.  check step. Highest Dunning Level, Oldest Open Item, and other  settings can be used as parameters for individual check steps. 

  8. Customer Group: Customers are grouped into various Customer Groups relevant for credit management. Standard groups such as Large Customer, Small/ Medium Customer, and Affiliate Company are available by default.
     
  9. Block in Credit Managment : Maintain a Credit Block in  UKM_BP (if not automatically maintained) under the Credit Segment Data  Credit Limit Tab / Control Data section with Reason Code.


    Notable References 

Reference 

Description 

Additional Comments  

 SAP Note 18613 

Checklist for Credit Management 

Instructions on how to review CM customizing for settings that may result in incorrect behavior. This Note applies to both the old and new (FSCM) credit management systems. 

Ashish Mohapatra 

 

Introducing Credit Management with SAP S/4HANA:

entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content: Hi @eric_festinger. I updated the question and shared sample parameter and values.

entry.title: Re: ASSERTION_FAILED
entry.content: the assret condition is violated

entry.title: Re: Missing Properties for Extension Object Collection
entry.content: Confirmed it was a bug. Fix in progress.

entry.title: Re: ASSERTION_FAILED
entry.content:
Category ABAP programming error
Runtime Errors ASSERTION_FAILED
ABAP Program XO_BUSINESS_FACTORY===========CP
Application Component CA-FS-XO
Date and Time 10.01.2025 22:24:34 (CET)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Short Text |
| The ASSERT condition was violated.

entry.title: Re: ASSERTION_FAILED
entry.content:
|method get_instance. |
| 2| |
| 3| data: |
| 4| lt_lo type table of cexo_lo, |
| 5| ls_bo type cexo_bo, |
| 6| ls_ref like line of ref_list, |
| 7| lv_tabix like sy-tabix, |
| 8| lcl_vo_factory type ref to xo_validation_factory. |
| 9| field-symbols: |
| 10| <lo> like line of lt_lo, |
| 11| <ref> like line of ref_list. |
| 12| |
| 13|* check contract violation: all parameters must be supplied |
| 14| assert: |
| 15| i_object_name is not initial, |
|>>>>>| i_object_key is not initial. |
| 17| |
| 18|* check, whether instance is in use |
| 19| read table ref_list assigning <ref> |
| 20| with table key |
| 21| bo_name = i_object_name |
| 22| object_key = i_object_key. |
| 23| |
| 24| if <ref> is assigned. |
| 25| r_instance = <ref>-reference. |
| 26| else. |
| 27| |
| 28| ls_ref-bo_name = i_object_name. |
| 29| ls_ref-object_key = i_object_key. |
| 30| |
| 31|* check, if a free recycleable instance is available |
| 32| read table recyclables assigning <ref> |
| 33| with key bo_name = i_object_name. |
| 34| |
| 35| if <ref> is assigned.

entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content: FIrst pass is for sure to resolve "contexts" and "keydates". Once answered, the "prompts" can be retrieved. However, getting the "contexts" should not take a long time, and I guess you mean that it is the 2nd pass which takes more time. Do you confirm?

entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter...
entry.content:
Hi 

To improve performance, fetch only the necessary parameters by adding filters or criteria
to the GET request, reducing the data size. Use parallel processing to split and handle large datasets simultaneously. Implement incremental updates to PUT only the parameters that have changed instead of sending all data every time. Optimize the API calls by adjusting timeouts, batching requests, or leveraging caching mechanisms. Finally, consult the SAP BO REST API documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/) for any performance-specific configurations.

entry.title: Alternative for report module once it's deprecated in CPQ
entry.content:
In the past there was a Reports section in setup that allowed users to retrieve quote data from the tool.
That section was removed, leaving the "Reports Module" section instead.
Now checking Help Portal page for the Reports Module, it says it is no longer being developed. What option will be available to use for reports?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Alternative for report module once it's deprecated in...
entry.content: Once Report module is deprecated, CPQ will add a feature with same functionality as current report feature, but inside CPQ application instead of using a third party component.

entry.title: Re: ASSERTION_FAILED
entry.content:
Category Dynpro runtime error
Runtime Errors DYNP_TOO_MANY_RADIOBUTTONS_ON
ABAP Program SAPLBUC0
Application Component AP-MD-BP
Date and Time 10.01.2025 22:17:41 (CET)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|Short Text |
| More than one radio button is active. |

entry.title: Re: Best Practices to Deploy MDK Application in Cloud Fou...
entry.content:
When you deploy to Mobile Services from BAS you first must login to CF and select the destination org and space corresponding to the Mobile Services Application where you want the metadata deployed to.  The deploy process will bundle up the metadata and send it to App Update under the specified mobile application.  You can go into the Mobile Services cockpit and select Mobile App Update to see the deployed version.

The Deploy process will also create the uploadBundle.zip in the .build directory under your application in BAS.  This is the bundled metadata that was automatically uploaded to App Update. You can also download this file and manually upload to App Update in the same or other instances (QA, Prod).

Deploy will not create a new instance in BTP of the mobile app.  You would need to either manually create the app in other environments (QA, Prod) or export the app from the Mobile Services cockpit and import to the other environments and make any necessary changes for the destinations etc. after importing.

In terms of your app working in Prod without deploying metadata to Prod it will depend on how the app was created in Prod as well as if your branded APK included branded metadata or not.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices to Deploy MDK Application in Cloud Fou...
entry.content: You can also use the mdk-tools CLI to deploy to the various environments as well

entry.title: Re: Data truncation with export to pdf
entry.content:
Hi 

To resolve the PDF formatting and data truncation issues, ensure that the font size discrepancy between the developer and server machines is addressed. Verify and align the font settings, including any custom fonts, across both environments. Check the Windows System Registry for any differences related to font rendering or scaling, and standardize these settings. Additionally, confirm that the SAP service pack upgrade applied all relevant patches and configurations correctly. If uncertainties persist regarding registry changes, consult SAP's documentation or support for precise guidance.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – December 2024
entry.content:
Could you please downport SQL Plan Advisor to HANA on prem as soon as possible? Cool Feature! It could help a lot of developers there.
(Who is using HANA Cloud for mission-critical development?)

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content:
Hi 

To update another BO through EML in a managed RAP object, avoid committing changes in `save_modified`. Use asynchronous business events to decouple the update and handle the EML operation in a separate transaction. Alternatively, store the required changes in a staging table or buffer and process them later via a background job. This ensures transaction consistency and avoids runtime errors.

entry.title: Re: Apache Kafka Connection from BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi 

Please check the below steps.

1. Get Kafka broker details (URL, port, topics) and authentication credentials (SASL/SSL if needed).                 2. Raise firewall clearance for SAP Integration Suite to access Kafka and check if Cloud Connector is required.                                                                                                                                                               3. Ensure Kafka topics are configured with proper permissions and share required certificates for secure access.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate geolocation in a MDK mobile applicat...
entry.content: What type of action will depend on your backend destination. If you are using an Odata backend then yes, use a CreateEntity or UpdateEntity action to store the coordinates based on your business requirements.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA: Configuration of Print Output for Maintenance Orders
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I am currently working on configuring the print output for maintenance orders in SAP S/4HANA. So far, I have completed the following configurations:

1) Cros-Application Components -> Output Control -> Manage Application Object Type Activation

2) Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Output Type

3) Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Output Channels

4) Maintenance and Service Processing -> Basic Settings- > Print Control -> Define Shop Papers, Forms and Output Programs

  • Define Shop Papers

  • Forms and Output Programs

When I try to execute the print output within the maintenance order, the system shows the interface attached.

I have also manually entered the required data, but the system still displays the errors shown in the figure.

What additional configurations are needed to ensure the print starts automatically without errors?

Thank you in advance for your help!

I have configurated the

entry.title: Re: How Can I Increase performance of Document Parameter Get And Put Request?
entry.content: @eric_festinger yes I getting the parameters with get request after that I am sending put request to getting all value this put request takes more time.

entry.title: Technical Issue with Appliance: SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA (Trial Version)
entry.content:
Dear SAP community ,

 

I am reaching out to report ongoing issues with the SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA (Trial Version) appliance. Despite multiple attempts to resolve the issue, the appliance remains in a failed state and is currently unusable. Below are the steps I have taken so far to address the problem:

1. AWS Integration and Security Group Configuration:

• Configured the appropriate security group rules for inbound and outbound traffic (e.g., ports 22, 443, 8080).

• The public IP address of my network has been correctly added to the rules.

2. Connection Testing via SSH and HTTPS:

• I successfully connected to the AWS instance, but the software status in the Cloud Appliance Library continues to show as “Failed”.

3. Recreating the Appliance:

• I terminated and recreated the appliance entirely, but the issue persists.

4. Logs and Diagnostics:

• I attempted to access the logs of the virtual machine but could not identify any specific details regarding the root cause of the failure.

 

Error Message and Additional Details:

• AWS Instance ID: i-06b1dd20d3be57d86

• Appliance Status: Active, but software status failed.

• Instance Public IP Address: 44.223.17.212

• AWS Region: us-east-1

• Logs: No relevant entries or specific indicators visible in the appliance interface or AWS console.

• Error Message in Appliance: “The software deployment failed on at least one virtual machine.

Additionally, I have attempted various configurations, including:

• Modifying the network settings,

• Reviewing AWS configurations,

• Restricting the IP range

Additional Information:

• Trial Version: SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA

• Access through SAP CAL: Yes

• Login Credentials: Registered via SAP Universal ID and AWS Cloud account.

 

Thank you in advance for your support. Please feel free to reach out if you require further details.

 

Kind regards,

Victor

entry.title: Problem updating delivery date (EINDT) and statistical delivery date (SLFDT) in PO badi
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Context:

Customer want's to update 3 fields with specific rules in schedule line  in ME22n. When he modifies  the delivery date (EINDT) he wants to add a certain number to this date, then he wants to cancel the standard redetermination of statiscal delivery date (SLFDT) when delivery date (EINDT is modified at screen. Finally he wants to put a certain value to ELDAT (Goods receipt date).

Problem: 

Method PROCESS_SCHEDULE of badi ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST is where i want to do my code as there are already things done for PO in this BADI. Let's say, the customer modifies delivery date from 10.01.2025 to 12.01.2025 and press ENTER. We want for example replace the 12.01.2025 with a specific rule which will lead to 15.01.2025 for EINDT. As said standard SLFDT is redetermined (note 248349) but we don't want so we set it to it's old value. Then we modifiy ELDAT with some other rule.

At the end of all of this, i check if there are differencies between LS_SCHEDULE_INIT that i loaded before doing anything and another structure LS_SCHEDULE with the possible modications. IF <> then i call the infamous SET_DATA( ls_schedule) and SET_DATA(ls_item) cause if i change the EINDT in schedule, i have also to change it in item...

This SET_DATA method triggers the reprocessing all modified objects (START method called) like SCHEDULE or ITEM and THERE IS my problem! The values that i saved at my first processing are lost when the PROCESS_SCHEDULE is triggered again, maybe because the standard have done some modifications, and i can't find a way to keep all my dermine values saved from on call to another cause values are sometimes not the same as at my first "run" in PROCESS_SCHEDULE. This is a nightmare to handle.

When several items are processed at a time, the nightmare is worse. PROCESS_SCHEDULE handles first ITEM 10 SCEDULE 0001, then ITEM 2 SCEDULE 0001 and if there were modification on one or another schedule, it's reprocessing everything in the same sequence and often wich modifications that i don't wanted...

Anyone faced the same problem? Again, I just wish to set values to EINDT SLFDT and ELDAT and tells the standard to stop messing the values i gave him.

Thanks a lot for your time.

Have a great day!

entry.title: Re: How to send Email Notifications only on the Last Retry Attempt in SAP CI/IS when using AEM
entry.content: Thank you, good detailed information is provided.

entry.title: Re: connect to mssql Database
entry.content:
From the Windows Start Menu type in ODBC Data Sources (64-bit)

Click on Add and select the 64 bit client, which you need to install if it's not there, and name the new DSN the same as your 32 bit DSN. CR will find it and carry one.

Talk to your IT guys on how to get it if you don't have it.

entry.title: Re: Problem updating delivery date (EINDT) and statistical delivery date (SLFDT) in PO badi
entry.content: Have you looked into the methods for retrieving the persistent data?

entry.title: Make keyfigure show no value
entry.content:
Hello community

In a report I want to create a keyfigure that when a price is displayed in the pricing condition, no value should be displayed in another pricing condition. We have managed to set the key figure to zero, but we have not yet found a way to make it completely empty. Does anyone know if this is possible?

entry.title: How to hide deleted contacts in CIC0 Transaction
entry.content: I have a requirement to not to show the contacts which are marked for deletion in CIC0 Tcode.  Suggest if any one have any idea in this

entry.title: Re: Connect PostgreSQL after SSH by CrystalReport
entry.content:
SSH is between the Client and Server, Crystal doesn't care how it connects it just assumes the connection is working.

Contact PostgreSQL on how to configure the Client.

Here are the supported Clients, note CR 2020 is BI 4.3 family:

PostgreSQLPostgreSQL (21)9.6ODBC  PostgreSQL 9.6 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.1 JDBC End of Support
10ODBC  PostgreSQL 10 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC   PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
11ODBC NewPostgreSQL 11 ODBC  End of Support
JDBC  NewPostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC End of Support
12ODBC BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 12 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP01+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct

 

13

ODBC BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP02+PostgreSQL 42.2 JDBC Direct
14ODBC BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP03+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct
15ODBC BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 13 ODBC  Direct
JDBC  BI 4.3 SP04+PostgreSQL 42.3 JDBC Direct

(21) Function and Procedure are relevant but different objects in PostgreSQL, only Function is supported in Crystal Reports. Functions are listed under "Stored Procedures", e.g., in CR ODBC driver. For more information, see https://me.sap.com/notes/3403784

entry.title: How to use Customer fields in HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG
entry.content:
Hi,

     I have created a z version of 'HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG' to ZH_HRESS_CC_GUIBBF_LEAVREQDIALOG but when I click send it shows the original dialog screen and not my version. Where do I configure the new screen?

 

Regards,

Vishal Agrawal
